title: penstemon crandallii subsp. taosensis
intro: Penstemon crandallii subsp. taosensis is a subspecies of the Penstemon crandallii plant, a type of flowering plant in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) that is native to the southwestern United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Penstemon crandallii subsp. taosensis include its potential to reduce fever, relieve respiratory issues, and protect against infections. Penstemon crandallii subsp. taosensis can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures for use in herbal remedies, although more research is needed to fully understand its properties and potential uses.
